name: IP Assignment — Contractor
text: The Assignor hereby assigns absolutely and irrevocably to the Assignee all right, title, and interest in and to all Intellectual Property, effective immediately upon the date of this Agreement.

legal_basis.note: Assignment must be in writing, signed by assignor
Patent assignment — must be in writing
Employer owns works made in employment — does NOT apply to independent contractors

invalid_if: The Intellectual Property sought to be assigned is not assignable under applicable law.
The Assignor does not possess the full right, title, and interest in the Intellectual Property to be assigned.
The term 'Intellectual Property' is not adequately defined within the contract.
source: Copyright Act, 1957 S.19; Patents Act, 1970 S.68
title: IP Assignment
dispute_hotspots: Copyright Act S.17 does not apply to contractors — client has NO IP rights without express written assignment
Pre-existing contractor IP incorporated into deliverable — client cannot claim ownership without specific assignment
